paper.title: Injuries in Japanese Junior Soccer Players During Games and Practices
paper.fileName: Kenji Kuzuhara and Masashi Shibata and Ryo Uchida - 2017 - Injuries in Japanese Junior Soccer Players During Games and Practices..pdf

operatorDecision.guidance: For multiline fields, use exactly 3 lines in this order: total/overall, matches/games, training/practices. Leave unreported rows blank rather than inferring. No illness data are reported in this paper, so illness tabs remain blank/not reported.

contextFields.label: Incidence Definition
contextFields.value: calculated as the total number of injuries per 1000 athlete-hours (AHs) of actual and game and practice time as well as per 1000 hours of exposure (sum of game and practice hours).

contextFields.label: Recurrence Definition
contextFields.value: A recurrent injury was an injury of the same type and at the same site as an index injury and occurred after a player had returned to full participation following the initial injury.

contextFields.label: Title
contextFields.value: Injuries in Japanese Junior Soccer Players During Games and Practices

injuryOutcome:injuryPlayersCompletedStud.label: Number of players that completed the study

injuryOutcome:injuryTeamsCompletedStudy.label: Number of teams that completed the study

Lower limb

Lower limb

chunks.label: Injury outcome
chunks.description: Extract the full injury outcome section in a single pass.

draftFields.sourceQuote: A total of 29 injuries occurred between April 2013 and March 2014.
draftFields.label: Total number of injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: Eighty-nine players in 5 community-based junior soccer club teams (U-12, age range = 11–12 years; U-11, age range = 10-11 years; U-10, age ≤10 years) in Nagoya, Japan participated in this study.
draftFields.label: Number of players that completed the study

draftFields.sourceQuote: Eighty-nine players in 5 community-based junior soccer club teams (U-12, age range = 11–12 years; U-11, age range = 10-11 years; U-10, age ≤10 years) in Nagoya, Japan participated in this study.
draftFields.label: Number of teams that completed the study

draftFields.sourceQuote: We defined an injury as any event, including a traumatic or overuse injury or a disease related to sport that met the following criteria²¹: (1) occurred during a regular game or practice, (2) led to a player missing any game or practice subsequent to the injury, and (3) caused the player to seek medical care from a physician or alternative medical specialist.
draftFields.label: Total medical attention injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: We defined an injury as any event, including a traumatic or overuse injury or a disease related to sport that met the following criteria²¹: (1) occurred during a regular game or practice, (2) led to a player missing any game or practice subsequent to the injury, and (3) caused the player to seek medical care from a physician or alternative medical specialist.
draftFields.label: Total time-loss injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: Of these, 16 injuries (55.2%) occurred during games and 13 injuries (44.8%) occurred during practices.
draftFields.label: Total match injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: Of these, 16 injuries (55.2%) occurred during games and 13 injuries (44.8%) occurred during practices. We defined an injury as any event... (3) caused the player to seek medical care from a physician or alternative medical specialist.
draftFields.label: Medical attention match injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: Of these, 16 injuries (55.2%) occurred during games and 13 injuries (44.8%) occurred during practices. We defined an injury as any event... (2) led to a player missing any game or practice subsequent to the injury
draftFields.label: Time-loss match injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: Of these, 16 injuries (55.2%) occurred during games and 13 injuries (44.8%) occurred during practices.
draftFields.label: Total training injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: Of these, 16 injuries (55.2%) occurred during games and 13 injuries (44.8%) occurred during practices. We defined an injury as any event... (3) caused the player to seek medical care from a physician or alternative medical specialist.
draftFields.label: Medical attention training injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: Of these, 16 injuries (55.2%) occurred during games and 13 injuries (44.8%) occurred during practices. We defined an injury as any event... (2) led to a player missing any game or practice subsequent to the injury
draftFields.label: Time-loss training injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: The overall injury rate was 2.59/1000 athlete-hours (AHs).
draftFields.label: Injury incidence (overall)

draftFields.sourceQuote: The game injury rate (GIR; 6.43/1000 AHs) was higher than the practice injury rate (PIR; 1.49/1000 AHs; P < .05).
draftFields.label: Match injury incidence

draftFields.sourceQuote: The game injury rate (GIR; 6.43/1000 AHs) was higher than the practice injury rate (PIR; 1.49/1000 AHs; P < .05).
draftFields.label: Training injury incidence

draftFields.sourceQuote: Table 2. Total injury rates, Total, IR (95% CI): 2.59 (1.65, 3.53)
draftFields.label: Injury incidence 95% CI

draftFields.sourceQuote: We did not collect information on injury severity (minor, moderate, major) or time to return to play because no medical staff, such as an athletic trainer or physical therapist, was available to each club team.
draftFields.label: Total time-loss due to injury

draftFields.sourceQuote: We did not collect information on injury severity (minor, moderate, major) or time to return to play because no medical staff, such as an athletic trainer or physical therapist, was available to each club team.
draftFields.label: Median time-loss

draftFields.sourceQuote: We did not collect information on injury severity (minor, moderate, major) or time to return to play because no medical staff, such as an athletic trainer or physical therapist, was available to each club team.
draftFields.label: Mean time-loss

draftFields.sourceQuote: With respect to types of overall injuries, contusions (n = 8, 27.6%) were the most frequent, followed by sprains, fractures, and wounds (each n = 5, 17.2%; Table 4). During games, contusions occurred most often (37.5%, 2.41/1000 AHs)... However, the most common types of injuries during practices were wounds (23.1%, 0.34/1000 AHs), followed by contusions and fractures (each 15.4%, 0.23/1000 AHs).
draftFields.label: Most common injury type

draftFields.value:
Lower limb
Lower limb
Lower limb
draftFields.sourceQuote: Regarding the anatomical areas of overall injuries, the lower limbs (n = 15, 51.7%) were affected most frequently... During games, the most common anatomical area of injury was the lower limb (62.5%, 4.02/1000 AHs)... During practices, the most frequent anatomical area of injury was the lower limb (38.5%, 0.57/1000 AHs)...
draftFields.label: Most common injury location

draftFields.sourceQuote: We did not collect information on injury severity (minor, moderate, major) or time to return to play because no medical staff, such as an athletic trainer or physical therapist, was available to each club team.
draftFields.label: Most common injury severity class

draftFields.sourceQuote: As for the mechanisms of overall injuries, other contact with the ball, field, or another object (n = 13, 44.8%) occurred most often, followed by body contact (n = 8, 27.6%; Table 5). Body contact (43.8%, 2.81/1000 AHs) was the most common injury mechanism during games, followed by other contact (37.5%, 2.41/1000 AHs), whereas during practices, other contact (53.8%, 0.83/1000 AHs) was most common, followed by body contact (7.7%, 0.11/1000 AHs).
draftFields.label: Contact

draftFields.sourceQuote: Table 5. Injury Rates by Injury Mechanism in Junior Soccer Teams in Japan, 2013-2014. No contact: Total (2), Games (2), Practice (0).
draftFields.label: Non-contact

draftFields.sourceQuote: We did not collect information on injury severity (minor, moderate, major) or time to return to play because no medical staff, such as an athletic trainer or physical therapist, was available to each club team.
draftFields.label: Median injury duration

draftFields.sourceQuote: We did not collect information on injury severity (minor, moderate, major) or time to return to play because no medical staff, such as an athletic trainer or physical therapist, was available to each club team.
draftFields.label: Mean injury duration (±SD)
